front: Novel: in this novel, the murdered miniaturist Elegant Effendi narrates the opening chapter, and other chapters are narrated by a gold coin, Satan, a drawing of a tree, and the color red
Novel: in this novel, Black investigates three miniaturists — Butterfly, Stork, and Olive — for the murder of Elegant Effendi while pursuing his cousin Shekure
Novel: in this novel, Master Osman blinds himself with a plume needle to paint from memory, inspired by the legend of the miniaturist Bihzad
Novel: in this novel, the murderer Olive is identified using the 'courtesan method' of examining horse nostrils painted in the Chinese style
Novel: in this novel, the poet Ka returns from exile in Frankfurt to the town of Kars to investigate suicides among religious girls prompted by a headscarf ban
Novel: in this novel, Kadife shoots the actor Sunay Zaim during a deadly performance of Kyd's The Spanish Tragedy
Novel: in this novel, an assassin steals 19 of the protagonist's 20 poems, whose titles like 'The Place Where God Does Not Exist' appear on a snowflake diagram
Novel: in this novel, the narrator 'Orhan Pamuk' reveals that the protagonist Ka was assassinated in Germany for betraying the militant Blue
Novel: in this novel, Kemal obsessively collects 4,213 cigarette butts and other objects belonging to the salesgirl Fusun after she dies in a car crash
Novel: in this novel, the protagonist first encounters Fusun while buying a handbag for his fiancee Sibel, and describes Fusun's butterfly-shaped earring as 'the happiest moment of his life'
Author: this author established a real-world Museum of Innocence in the Cukurcuma district of Istanbul, displaying 4,213 cigarette butts among other items
Novel: in this novel, an Italian scholar is kidnapped by pirates and enslaved by the Ottoman scholar Hoja, who looks exactly like him and is obsessed with building a 'war engine' for the sultan
Novel: in this novel, the lawyer Galip searches for his missing wife Ruya and impersonates the missing journalist Jelal, whose newspaper columns form the even-numbered chapters
Novel: this novella contrasts Ferdowsi's Rostam and Sohrab (filicide) with Sophocles' Oedipus (patricide) through the story of a well-digger's apprentice who drops a bucket onto his boss's head
Author: this Turkish author was prosecuted under Article 301 for 'insulting Turkishness' after discussing the Armenian Genocide in the Swiss magazine Das Magazin
Novel: this novel opens 'I read a book one day and my whole life changed' and features Dr. Fine, who leads a conspiracy to assassinate readers of the title book
